_comment: Example AIP Engagement Object — AIP spec §4.8.1. This example models a procurement engagement between a hiring operator (enterprise platform) and a deploying principal (Alice's organisation). Two agents participate: an orchestrator agent and a payment-processing service agent. An approval gate requires Alice's sign-off before payment execution. Signatures are placeholders.
title: Q2 2026 Office Supplies Procurement — Alice Corp

payload.title: Q2 2026 Office Supplies Procurement — Alice Corp

_notes.bi_lateral_activation: proposed→active requires BOTH hiring_operator_signature and deploying_principal_signature. The change_log records engagement_created (seq:1) then engagement_countersigned (seq:2) to document this two-party activation.
_notes.capability_overlay: The payment-processor participant has capability_overlay_id pointing to the overlay in capability-overlay.json, which restricts its transaction limits to £500/day for this engagement.
_notes.approval_gate: The 'payment-approval' gate is 'pending'. Credential Token validations for actions scoped to this engagement that trigger this gate will fail with engagement_gate_pending until Alice approves it.
_notes.append_only_log: change_log is append-only. The Registry MUST reject any modification of existing entries. Only seq values strictly greater than 3 are valid for new entries.
_notes.termination_cascade: If terminated: (1) all Capability Overlays for this engagement_id are invalidated, (2) all pending/approved/executing Approval Envelopes scoped to this engagement_id are cancelled, (3) subsequent Credential Token validation fails with engagement_terminated.
